title: Knowledge Fragmentation
question: Why are product development teams repeatedly solving the same problems despite hiring experienced employees and investing heavily in collaboration tools?
context: Benchmark case designed to test whether Discovery can infer knowledge fragmentation from duplicated work, scattered documentation, weak knowledge transfer, organizational memory loss, and weakening organizational continuity.

evidence.title: Engineering Retrospective
evidence.content: Three separate product teams independently developed nearly identical internal software tools without realizing similar solutions already existed elsewhere in the company.

evidence.title: Employee Survey
evidence.content: 68% of employees report they often struggle to locate existing documentation or determine whether previous work has already addressed a problem.

evidence.title: Research Division Review
evidence.content: Scientists frequently repeat experiments because earlier findings are difficult to locate or are stored in personal notebooks rather than shared repositories.

evidence.title: New Employee Interviews
evidence.content: Recently hired employees report it takes several months before they know who possesses critical expertise across the organization.

evidence.title: Customer Support Analysis
evidence.content: Support teams regularly escalate issues to engineering because historical resolutions cannot be easily found in existing knowledge systems.

evidence.title: Product Development Audit
evidence.content: Multiple teams maintain separate documentation systems with inconsistent formats and little cross-referencing between departments.

evidence.title: Executive Strategy Meeting
evidence.content: Leadership noted that valuable organizational knowledge often leaves when experienced employees resign or transfer internally.

evidence.title: Project Postmortem
evidence.content: A recently completed product launch encountered technical problems that had already been solved during a previous launch two years earlier, but those lessons were never reused.

evidence.title: IT Infrastructure Review
evidence.content: The organization currently stores operational knowledge across shared drives, cloud folders, personal notes, email threads, Slack channels, and local documents without a unified indexing strategy.

evidence.title: Innovation Program Review
evidence.content: Teams frequently describe discovering useful internal knowledge by accident through informal conversations rather than through organizational systems.

compressedConcepts: not reliably preserving what it learns
